steps.title: Join two complete clauses
steps.content: The sentence joins two independent clauses: the calculators were popular in the first half of the twentieth century, and the devices were eventually replaced. The contrast between popularity and replacement calls for a comma plus a coordinating conjunction.

steps.title: Reject faulty joins
steps.content:
• Choice A gives no punctuation or conjunction between two complete clauses.
  • Choice B uses only a comma, creating a comma splice.
  • Choice D uses "that" in a way that makes the sentence ungrammatical.
The remaining choice correctly joins the clauses and signals contrast. The answer is C.
